patch.value: GH-504: importer/exporter registered without options yields an unusable CLI command (no arg/flag contracts)

When an extension registers an importer/exporter with only a handler — api.registerImporter('todos', handler) — and omits the optional options argument, the derived '<name> import'/'<name> export' CLI command is completely unusable: positional args are rejected ('Too many arguments'), every flag is rejected ('Unknown option'), and --help shows no flags and no <file> arg. The same handler works via the SDK/MCP action path (runAction with options). Nothing warns the author at registration, doctor has no diagnostic, and the runtime errors give an agent no actionable hint. Adjacent to GH-503 (pm-s9iu) but a distinct code path: here the CANONICAL derived command of an installed importer/exporter carries no contract at all. Candidate remedies from the issue: pass-through/permissive arg mode for contract-less importer commands, a registration-time or doctor warning, and an error hint pointing at the SDK action path.

Bare importer/exporter registrations produce a usable CLI command (positional file arg + generic flag pass-through) OR registration/doctor surfaces an actionable warning and the runtime error names the missing-options cause and the working SDK action path; regression test covers a handler-only registerImporter fixture; GH-504 closed with a comment linking the fix
